1(1) | CSNOBOL4B 2.3.4 | April 24, 2026

Project Traverse Tree

Project Information

Project: https://github.com/ad2108/traverse-binary-tree-in-snobol4
Author: ad2108
Date: 04.07.2026
Version: 1.0.0
License: MIT

Documentation

Null Constant

    null = 'NULL'
null is used as a simple pattern, that can be matched against.

Node Datatype

    data('node(value,left,right)')
This datatype is used for all the nodes in a binary tree. It contains the value and to references to 2 further nodes.

Nullnode

    nullnode = node(null,null,null)
Null Node is used for finishing/missing nodes in the tree. All elements of the null node contain the constant null.

Expression Tree as an Example

In this code example the expression sin(8) + 3 - log(5) is used. To get a working result all empty and follow up nodes need to be nullnodes!

5 -> node(5,nullnode,nullnode)

2+3 -> node('+',node(2,nullnode,nullnode),node(3,nullnode,nullnode))

Function Preorder

The preoder function is a recursive function that traverses a given binary tree in preoder. This means that the order of traversion is:
  1. Root
  2. Left/-subtree
  3. Right/-subtree

Function Inorder

The inorder function is a recursive function that traverses a given binary tree in inorder. This means that the order of traversion is:
  1. Left/-subtree
  2. Root
  3. Right/-subtree

Function Postorder

The postorder function is a recursive function that traverses a given binary tree in postorder. This means that the order of traversion is:
  1. Left/-subtree
  2. Right/-subtree
  3. Root

Main Section

The main section runs all 3 traversion strategies on the expression tree defined earlier.

Output

Traverse in Preoder
+
sin
8
-
3
log
5

Traverse in Inorder
8
sin
+
3
log
5
-

Traverse in Postorder
8
sin
5
log
3
-
+

End of Documentation
